data.title: ActiveCampaign Tutorial: Automate Email in 5 Steps
data.excerpt: This ActiveCampaign tutorial walks you through building email automations from scratch. Follow 5 clear steps and start converting smarter today.

$media.alt: ActiveCampaign Tutorial: Automate Email in 5 Steps

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you purchase through them, TonyCiencia may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*

I remember the first time I opened ActiveCampaign. I had a list of 2,000 subscribers, a clunky spreadsheet tracking who bought what, and a single "blast" email that went out every Tuesday at 10 AM. My open rates hovered around 14%. My click-throughs were embarrassing.

Six weeks later — after replacing that spreadsheet with ActiveCampaign's CRM and swapping my Tuesday blasts for behavior-triggered automations — my open rates hit 38% and revenue per email tripled. Not because I worked harder. Because the platform did the heavy lifting.

That transformation is exactly what this tutorial will help you replicate. Whether you're migrating from another tool or starting fresh, I'll walk you through five concrete steps to get ActiveCampaign running for your business. No fluff. Just the actions that actually move the needle.
Ready to see what this platform can do for you?
Try ActiveCampaign free for 14 days
and follow along.
Step 1: Create Your Account and Pick the Right Plan
Head to
activecampaign.com
and click "Start a free trial." You don't need a credit card — just your name, email, and business info.
During onboarding, ActiveCampaign asks a few smart questions:
How many contacts do you have?
This determines your pricing tier. Plans start around $29/month for up to 500 contacts and scale from there.
What's your primary goal?
(Nurture leads, sell products, retain customers.) Your answer pre-configures your dashboard with relevant templates and suggested automations.
What platform are you migrating from?
If you're switching from Mailchimp, ConvertKit, or another tool, ActiveCampaign offers guided import wizards that preserve your tags, segments, and historical engagement data.
**Pro tip from the
ActiveCampaign tips guide

:** Don't overthink the plan selection. You can upgrade at any time, and the Starter plan includes automations, segmentation, and the CRM — which is more than enough to see real results within your trial.
Once you land in the dashboard, take two minutes to:
1. Verify your sending domain (Settings → Domains). This dramatically improves deliverability.
2. Connect your website or e-commerce platform via the integrations menu. Shopify, WordPress, WooCommerce, and 900+ other tools plug in natively.
Step 2: Import Contacts and Build Smart Segments
This is where most people rush — and it costs them later. Spend ten extra minutes here and every email you send will be sharper.
Import your list:

Go to Contacts → Import. You can upload a CSV, paste from a spreadsheet, or pull directly from another platform. Map your columns to ActiveCampaign fields: email (required), first name, last name, purchase history, lead source — whatever data you have.
Create tags immediately.
Tags are the backbone of everything in ActiveCampaign. Examples:
`buyer-product-A` — purchased your flagship course
`lead-webinar-oct` — registered for your October webinar
`engaged-last-30` — opened or clicked in the past month
Now build segments.
Navigate to Contacts → Segments and combine tags with behavioral filters. For instance:
> "Contacts tagged 'lead-webinar-oct' who did NOT tag 'buyer-product-A' and visited my pricing page in the last 7 days."
That segment represents warm leads who need one final nudge — and you can target them with surgical precision. If you want a deeper dive into segmentation strategies, check out our
complete ActiveCampaign use cases breakdown
Step 3: Design Your First Email Campaign
Go to Campaigns → Create a campaign. You have four types to choose from:
Standard
— a one-time broadcast (your classic newsletter)
Automated
— triggered by an event inside an automation
RSS-Triggered
— sends whenever your blog publishes new content
Split Testing
— A/B tests subject lines, content, or send times
For your first campaign, pick
Standard
. Here's what makes ActiveCampaign's email builder stand out:
Drag-and-drop, but smarter.
You're not just arranging boxes. The builder suggests block types based on your goal: product showcase, countdown timer, social proof, personalized recommendations pulling from your CRM data.
Conditional content.
This feature alone is worth the switch. You can show different blocks to different segments *within the same email.* Imagine sending one campaign where:
Subscribers in the US see a free shipping banner
Subscribers in Europe see a local pricing offer
New contacts see a "Welcome" header
Returning customers see a "Welcome back" header
All in one send. One subject line. One audience. Multiple personalized experiences.
Explore what ActiveCampaign can do
for a full feature overview.
Dynamic personalization goes deep.
Beyond "Hi {first_name}," you can insert deal values, last product purchased, custom field data, and even predicted likelihood to purchase using ActiveCampaign's AI-driven predictive sending.
Step 4: Build Your First Automation (This Is Where the Magic Happens)
If email campaigns are the engine, automations are the entire vehicle — steering, accelerating, and braking without you touching the wheel.
Go to Automations → Create an automation → Start from scratch.
The visual builder is genuinely best-in-class.
You'll see a flowchart-style canvas where you drag triggers, actions, and conditions onto a timeline. Here's a simple but powerful automation to build right now:
"Post-Purchase Nurture" Automation:
Trigger:
Contact tag added → `buyer-product-A`
Wait:
1 day
Action:
Send email → "How to get the most from Product A" (value-driven tutorial)
Wait:
3 days
If/Else:
Has contact opened the tutorial email?
Yes →
Send email → "Advanced tips + invite to community"
No →
Send email → "Did you miss this? Quick start guide" (different subject line, same content)
Wait:
5 days
Action:
Send email → "Would you recommend us?" (review request)
Goal:
Contact tagged `review-given` → exit automation
That's a complete post-purchase experience running on autopilot. Every buyer gets a tailored journey based on their engagement. You set it up once.
Branching logic is unlimited.

You can add splits based on tags, custom fields, deal stages, site visits, email engagement, geolocation, date triggers, score changes, and even webhook responses. The depth rivals platforms that charge 5–10x more. Our
why choose ActiveCampaign guide
explains exactly how this compares favorably to enterprise tools.
Get started with ActiveCampaign's free trial
and build this automation today.
Step 5: Track Performance, Test, and Optimize
ActiveCampaign's reporting suite is refreshingly transparent. After your first campaigns go out:
Email reports
show opens, clicks, unsubscribes, and — this matters — a per-contact engagement timeline. You can literally see *which links* a specific subscriber clicked across every email they've received.
Automation reports
reveal where contacts drop off in your flows. If 40% of people never open the email at Step 3, you know exactly what to fix.
Revenue attribution
(on Plus plans and above) ties email activity directly to purchases. No guessing which campaign drove the sale.
Run split tests constantly.

Test subject lines on every broadcast. Test send times. Test whether a plain-text email outperforms your designed template for a given segment. ActiveCampaign's split-test node inside automations even lets you A/B test entire automation branches — send 50% of new leads down a short nurture and 50% down a long one, then compare conversion rates after 30 days.
The feedback loop is fast. Within two weeks of active sending, you'll have enough data to make meaningful changes.
New and Standout Features Worth Knowing About
ActiveCampaign ships updates frequently. Here are the features that have the biggest impact right now:
Active Intelligence (AI-powered):

The platform now includes AI that suggests subject lines, predicts the best send time for each individual contact, and identifies at-risk subscribers before they churn. It's not gimmicky — it directly lifts engagement metrics.
Landing pages built in:
No need for a separate tool. Drag-and-drop landing pages live inside ActiveCampaign, connected directly to your forms, automations, and CRM.
SMS messaging:
Send text messages as standalone campaigns or weave them into automations alongside email. A flash sale reminder hitting someone's phone 30 minutes after an email can double your conversion rate.
Sales pipeline CRM:

Manage deals, assign tasks, and track pipeline stages without leaving the platform. This is especially useful for coaches, consultants, and agencies who need both marketing automation and sales tracking.
900+ integrations:
Shopify, Stripe, Salesforce, Slack, WordPress, Calendly, Zapier — if your stack uses it, ActiveCampaign connects to it.
Stay current with our regularly updated
ActiveCampaign new features roundup
Who Is ActiveCampaign Ideal For?
This platform isn't trying to be everything for everyone. But for these groups, it's a near-perfect fit:
Small to midsize businesses
that want enterprise-grade automation without enterprise-grade pricing. Plans scale with your contact list, and even the entry tier includes features competitors lock behind expensive add-ons.
E-commerce store owners
who need abandoned cart sequences, post-purchase nurture flows, and product recommendation emails triggered by browsing behavior. The Shopify and WooCommerce integrations are seamless.
Course creators and coaches
selling digital products. ActiveCampaign handles launch sequences, webinar follow-ups, student onboarding, and upsell funnels inside a single platform.
Agencies managing multiple clients.
The Agency plan lets you white-label and manage separate client accounts from one dashboard.
Anyone graduating from basic email tools.
If you've outgrown Mailchimp or ConvertKit and need real automation logic, conditional content, and CRM-grade contact management, this is your next step.
Your Quick-Start Checklist
Here's what to do in your first 48 hours:
1. ✅ Start your
free 14-day ActiveCampaign trial
2. ✅ Verify your sending domain
3. ✅ Import your contact list and apply tags
4. ✅ Create 2–3 core segments
5. ✅ Build one automation (post-purchase or welcome sequence)
6. ✅ Send your first campaign and watch the data roll in
That's it. No six-week onboarding program. No consultant required. ActiveCampaign is powerful enough to grow with you for years, and approachable enough to deliver value on day one.
The best marketing automation tool is the one you actually use — and ActiveCampaign makes it remarkably easy to start using it well.

children.text: Is ActiveCampaign free to use?

ActiveCampaign doesn't offer a free plan, but it provides a 14-day free trial with no credit card required. The trial gives you full access to core features including automations, email campaigns, segmentation, and the CRM so you can test everything before committing. Paid plans start at around $29/month depending on your contact list size.
How long does it take to set up ActiveCampaign?

Most users can get their account live within a single afternoon. Domain verification takes about 10 minutes, contact imports are instant via CSV or native integrations, and building your first automation using the drag-and-drop builder can take as little as 30 minutes. The platform's onboarding wizard walks you through each step.
Can ActiveCampaign replace my current CRM?

Yes. ActiveCampaign includes a full sales CRM with deal pipelines, contact scoring, task management, and lead tracking built directly into the platform. For many small and midsize businesses, it eliminates the need for a separate CRM tool entirely because your marketing data and sales data live in one unified contact record.
